original_abstract: Amidst the ongoing wave of digital transformation, blockchain technology has emerged as a robust framework for addressing systemic issues surrounding interoperability, data privacy, and resource optimization. By providing a decentralized architecture, this technology holds the potential to fundamentally revolutionize modern healthcare delivery. This study investigates the transformative capacity of blockchain within the medical sector, exploring how it serves as a strategic catalyst for overcoming long-standing structural obstacles. Adhering to the PRISMA methodology, we conducted a systematic review of ten pivotal studies centered on the design and execution of blockchain-based solutions. Our analysis highlights critical applications in data security, pharmaceutical supply chain integrity, and cross-institutional data sharing. Ultimately, this paper provides a comprehensive synthesis of the current landscape of blockchain in healthcare, weighing its substantial clinical and administrative benefits against the inherent challenges of large-scale implementation.
humanized_abstract: In the era of digital transformation, blockchain technology provides a solution to the problems faced by interoperability, data security and resource utilization in the existing systems. Blockchain technology, as a decentralized system, has the potential to revolutionize the existing healthcare system. Therefore, this study aims at identifying the change brought about by the use of blockchain technology in the medical and healthcare sector and how it acts as a change strategy to overcome the structural barriers of healthcare. Based on the PRISMA guidelines, this systematic review reviewed ten core studies relating to the development and implementation of blockchain in healthcare. The core studies were based on the applications of blockchain in providing data protection, anti-counterfeit drug tracking in pharmaceutical industry and safe healthcare information exchange among the hospitals. This paper provides an integrated summary of the current applications and developments of blockchain technology in healthcare systems, examining the clinical and managerial benefits and challenges associated with its adoption at the health system level.
